name: Remote Service Activity via SVCCTL Named Pipe
notes:
This rule detects remote service activity via remote access to the svcctl named pipe.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: mdecrevoisier
SIGMA Title: Remote service creation via named pipes
SIGMA Description: Detects scenarios where an attacker remotely creates a service over named pipes. Tools like Tchopper or CrackMapExec can trigger this rule.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001471) Success Audit: Network Share Object Checked for Access
when the event matches Share Name (custom) is any of IPC$
when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of svcctl
when the event matches Accesses (custom) contains any of WriteData
TD_list: APPLY Remote Service Activity via SVCCTL Named Pipe on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001471) Success Audit: Network Share Object Checked for Access
AND when the event matches Share Name (custom) is any of IPC$
AND when the event matches Filename (custom) is any of svcctl
AND when the event matches Accesses (custom) contains any of WriteData
RED_list: Event name: Remote Service Activity via SVCCTL Named Pipe
Event description: Detects remote service activity via remote access to the svcctl named pipe.
